name: Castejón — circular from Cañaveruelas
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cañaveruelas offers routes through a landscape characterized by reservoirs, rolling hills, and rural Spanish countryside. The region features varied terrain, with routes often circling large water bodies like the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Buendía Reservoir. Elevation changes are present, ranging from gentle gradients to more challenging climbs, providing diverse experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Cañaveruelas The most popular touring cycling route is Azud de Pareja from Sacedón — circular by the Embalse de Entrepeñas, a 30.7 miles (49.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the Embalse de Entrepeñas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Route of the Faces and Buendía Reservoir — circular from Buendía, a moderate 14.6 miles (23.5 km) path. This route explores unique rock formations and the shores of the Buendía Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Castejón — circular from Cañaveruelas, a 24.1 miles (38.8 km) trail leading through the local countryside around Cañaveruelas, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Cañaveruelas is defined by expansive reservoir views, undulating rural roads, and local village connections. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cañaveruelas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Cañaveruelas area?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 10 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Cañaveruelas, offering a variety of experiences through the region's landscapes. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes around Cañaveruelas?
A: Yes, there is at least &lt;strong&gt;1 easy touring cycling route&lt;/strong&gt; available. While the region features varied terrain, options exist for those seeking a more relaxed ride. Look for routes with lower elevation changes.

Q: What about more challenging touring cycling routes in Cañaveruelas?
A: For experienced touring cyclists, Cañaveruelas offers &lt;strong&gt;6 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and longer distances, providing a good challenge. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189332803/alhondiga-y-aunon-desde-sacedon-circular-por-el-embalse-del-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhóndiga y Auñón desde Sacedón — circular por el Embalse del Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 64 km with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Cañaveruelas?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Cañaveruelas are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1095106500/castejon-circular-from-canaveruelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castejón — circular from Cañaveruelas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that takes you through the local countryside.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cañaveruelas?
A: Touring cycling in Cañaveruelas is characterized by &lt;strong&gt;expansive reservoir views&lt;/strong&gt;, particularly of the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Buendía Reservoir, undulating rural roads, and traditional Spanish countryside. You'll often find routes circling these large water bodies, offering picturesque vistas.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You can explore the unique rock formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; near the Buendía Reservoir, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Guadiela River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views.

Q: Is parking available near touring cycling trailheads in Cañaveruelas?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't always detailed, most villages and towns in the Cañaveruelas region, such as Sacedón or Buendía, offer public parking areas where you can leave your vehicle before starting your ride. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Cañaveruelas?
A: The region's climate, with its reservoirs and rural landscapes, generally makes &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn ideal&lt;/strong&gt; for touring cycling. Temperatures are milder, and the scenery is often at its best. Summer can be quite hot, especially midday, while winter might bring cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Cañaveruelas?
A: While the guide highlights routes of varying difficulties, families looking for easier rides should focus on the &lt;strong&gt;moderate or easy-rated routes&lt;/strong&gt;, which tend to have less elevation gain and shorter distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086262219/route-of-the-faces-and-buendia-reservoir-circular-from-buendia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces and Buendía Reservoir — circular from Buendía&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a moderate 23.5 km route that could be suitable for families with some cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cañaveruelas?
A: Over 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cañaveruelas. They often praise the &lt;strong&gt;expansive reservoir views&lt;/strong&gt; and the varied terrain that offers both gentle gradients and challenging climbs. The region's quiet rural roads are also frequently highlighted as a positive aspect of the cycling experience.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near small villages and towns where you can find local cafes, bars, or small shops to refuel and refresh. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer stretches between settlements, as services can be sparse in some rural areas.
tips: The ruins of the Roman city of Ercávica from the year 179 B.C. approximately. Free visit until June 30, 2023 (normal entry 2 eur). There is no toilet.

A small town where you will find a supermarket and two bakeries. Stroll through its alleys and admire the surroundings from the "Balcón de La Alcarria" viewpoint, which since 2017 has been given the name "José Luís Perales Viewpoint" by the Villa de Castejón in recognition of his artistic and professional career.
